title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
summary: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.
writeup: Julia Turc interviews Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq, about the company's LPU architecture, a hardware chip built specifically for LLM inference. The discussion compares the LPU to GPUs using Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and covers a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. Technical topics include static scheduling and how the design handles mixture-of-experts models, alongside the broader theme of rising token demand.
